loyalty_points.earning_rule: Passengers earn 1 loyalty point for every 1 USD spent on completed national rail bookings.
loyalty_points.redemption_rule: 100 loyalty points can be redeemed for a 1.00 USD discount on eligible national rail bookings.
loyalty_points.eligibility: Only registered users are eligible to earn and redeem loyalty points.
loyalty_points.limitations: Points cannot reduce the fare below 0 USD. Points cannot be exchanged for cash. Only one redemption per booking is allowed.
loyalty_points.how_to_check: Use the check_loyalty_points command or ask the assistant: How many loyalty points do I have?

membership_tiers.current_implementation: All registered users belong to a single membership tier with the same earning and redemption rates.
membership_tiers.future_tiers: Future versions may introduce Silver, Gold, and Platinum tiers with enhanced earning multipliers and higher per-booking discount caps.

transfer_waiting_penalty.description: Routes involving interchanges between City Metro and National Rail networks may include a transfer waiting penalty.
transfer_waiting_penalty.purpose: The penalty accounts for the walking time between platforms, waiting for the next service, and potential crowding at busy interchange stations.
transfer_waiting_penalty.how_it_works: A fixed transfer waiting time of 4 minutes is added to the total journey time for each interchange on the route.

crowded_station_penalty.description: Busy interchange stations receive a small additional crowding penalty when calculating adjusted journey scores.
crowded_station_penalty.affected_stations: Central Station (MS01/NR01) is the busiest interchange and receives a higher crowding penalty of 1.5 minutes.
crowded_station_penalty.other_interchanges: Old Town (MS07/NR03) and Ferndale (MS15/NR07) receive a lower crowding penalty of 0.5 minutes.
crowded_station_penalty.how_to_avoid: Ask the assistant to find a route while avoiding crowded transfers to see the adjusted journey score.
